title.name: Standing Light Punch
title.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; great range for a chainable 5f jab (but slightly shorter than a standard shoto 5LK); DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd recovery frame; Sagat's only light normal that combos naturally into 214LK

title.name: Standing Medium Punch
title.notes: Core pressure tool for frame traps (5MPx2 sets up a trade combo vs. most reversal 4f normals); pushback on block is too far for a throw mixup without Drive Rush; links consistently into 2MP on counter-hit or into 5HK on Punish Counter (very strong Drive Reversal punish option); on regular hit can lead to 5MP, 5MP, 5LP combo

title.name: Standing Heavy Punch
title.notes: 3f extra recovery on whiff; huge range poke with a hitconfirmable TC follow-up; the first 10 recovery frames extend a wide arm-height hurtbox making it easily whiff punished by standing normals

title.name: Standing Light Kick
title.notes: Long range but slow startup (more comparable to a medium normal); 2f extra recovery on whiff; great whiff punish tool that combos consistently into 623HP on Punish Counter; input overlap with 6LK Step Low Kick makes it harder to walk forward and poke in neutral; DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd recovery frame

title.name: Standing Medium Kick
title.notes: Very long poke with a semi-hitconfirmable TC follow-up; more range vs. standing opponents (the tip of the hitbox whiffs on crouching opponents); the first 10 recovery frames extend a wide leg hurtbox making it more easily whiff punished by standing normals

title.name: Standing Heavy Kick
title.notes: Upper Body/Leg hurtbox is anti-air invincible on frames 9-15 (solid anti-air against midrange jumps); cannot hit cross-up; 1st hit forces stand on hit; 2nd hit whiffs on crouching opponents (2f worse on block); 4f extra recovery if both hits whiff; 1st hit deals extra hitstun, allowing a natural combo into 214HK; deals full damage / Drive damage / Meter gain even if only the 2nd hit connects; 1st hit puts airborne opponents into a free juggle state (limited juggle if comboed from a wallsplat or airborne crumple), then air resets on the 2nd hit; 2nd hit only has juggle potential after the 1st hit connects; the first 11 recovery frames extend a wide leg hurtbox making it more easily whiff punished by standing normals; carries counter-hit/Punish Counter advantage through both hits

title.name: Crouching Light Punch
title.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; excellent range for a 4f jab; high pushback makes tick throw and counter-hit pressure weaker, but also allows Sagat to space himself outside the opponent's reversal normals on block; DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd~3rd recovery frame; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-5 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

title.name: Crouching Medium Punch
title.notes: Solid mid-range cancelable poke and buffer tool; 1f extra recovery on block; 3f extra recovery on whiff; significantly more range on active frames 2-3 (more effective linking from +8 or punishing -8 moves); cancelable into 2 different Target Combo follow-ups with different hitconfirm timings (requires Punish Counter or Drive Rush for 2MP~HP combo); topmost hurtbox is vulnerable only to aerial strikes on frames 1-9 (preventing its use as an anti-air), and is projectile invincible on frames 1-14 to avoid High Tiger Shot (frames 15-26 are still vulnerable)

title.name: Crouching Heavy Punch
title.notes: Very long range for a cancelable and hitconfirmable poke; significantly more range on active frames 3-4; topmost hurtbox is vulnerable only to aerial strikes on frames 1-14 (preventing its use as an anti-air), and is projectile invincible on frames 1-19 to avoid High Tiger Shot (frames 20-35 are still vulnerable)

title.name: Crouching Light Kick
title.notes: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; low starter that can combo into any 3-light chain from close range

title.name: Crouching Medium Kick
title.notes: Amazing hitbox priority as a neutral poke; can link to 5LP at closer ranges, or into 2MP/5LK on Counter-hit; useful combo/pressure starter out of Drive Rush (but has poor link options near its max range)

title.name: Tiger Kick
title.notes: Has some juggle potential; great range and hitbox priority; can be punished on block by most other sweeps in the game

title.name: Jumping Light Punch
title.notes: Fast air-to-air with solid horizontal range

title.name: Jumping Medium Punch
title.notes: 2 hits; puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state; if 1st hit is stand blocked, the 2nd hit will not hit overhead against crouch block; lower body projectile invuln 8-22f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

title.name: Jumping Heavy Punch
title.notes: Strong downward attack angle; spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents; lower body projectile invuln 10-22f (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

title.name: Jumping Light Kick
title.notes: Cross-up; can be used as a fuzzy instant overhead

title.name: Jumping Medium Kick
title.notes: Long horizontal range (barely shorter than j.HK) with a better downward attack angle and nearly identical hitstun and blockstun; lower body projectile invuln 8-25f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

title.name: Jumping Heavy Kick
title.notes: Longest horizontal range; puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state; lower body projectile invuln 10-21f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

title.name: Tiger Heavy Elbow
title.notes: Spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents; Drive Rush gives a combo into 2MP (no real strike/throw pressure on block); meaty timing allows a follow-up link (up to +5/0 on final active frame)

title.name: Tiger Monolith
title.notes: Tall hitbox on 2nd hit, but also extends a tall hurtbox with no anti-air invincibility (likely to lose/trade with jump-ins); cannot hit cross-up; 2nd hit whiffs on crouch blocking opponents (3f worse on block and not cancelable); cancel does not occur until the 2nd hit's active frames (still executes on hit even if the 2nd hit whiffs); puts opponents into a limited juggle state (can combo into 236MP, DR~delay 4HP > 236HK, 623MP for great midscreen damage and corner carry); free juggle state from DR~4HP, allowing a more optimal corner juggle: > 214MK, 4HP > 236HK, 623MP; launches higher on Counter-hit/Punish Counter (KD +41) or on airborne opponents (KD +40); 1st hit puts opponent into a free juggle OTG bounce state that is usually overwritten by the 2nd hit (but can still occur on trades or when hitting extended hurtboxes)

title.name: Step Low Kick
title.notes: Long range advancing low poke that leaves Sagat close for a strike/throw mixup on hit while remaining safe on block; 3f extra recovery on whiff; can link into light normals on Counter-hit, or into 2MP after Drive Rush or Punish Counter; knocks down airborne opponents (KD +35~38) when juggled into (but has no juggle potential on its own); the first 7 recovery frames extend a wide foot hurtbox making it more easily whiff punished by low-reaching normals

title.name: Step High Kick
title.notes: Upper Body/Leg hurtbox is anti-air invincible on frames 14-19 (useful as a predictive long range anti-air); whiffs on crouching opponents; puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state; Punish Counter staggers grounded opponents and puts airborne opponents into a free juggle state; 3f fewer total frames on block/parry/armor (active frames end upon contact, with variable recovery depending on which active frame connected); the first 5 recovery frames extend a wide hurtbox where the kicking leg was (too high for many attacks to easily connect as a whiff punish); extends hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles; useful in Drive Rush cancel routes from 2HP/5HK/2MP~HP (but the pushback prevents Sagat's usual midscreen link of 4HP > 236MP, juggle DR~4HP)

title.name: Step Middle Kick
title.notes: 3f blockstring gap between hits; somewhat hitconfirmable knockdown from a very strong midrange poke; puts opponent into a limited juggle state (can combo 623LP after DI wallsplat on hit)

title.name: Tiger Sting
title.notes: Both hits are always a true blockstring; hitconfirmable knockdown from a very strong midrange poke; sets up a KD +42 safe jump when juggled into (like after a DI wallsplat on hit/block)

title.name: Tiger Slash
title.notes: Combos after Punish Counter or Drive Rush 2MP only; causes limited juggle OTG bounce vs. airborne opponents

title.name: Tiger Rise
title.notes: 3f blockstring gap between hits; harder to confirm into from 2MP but combos naturally; launch only allows a canceled Super but no other follow-up juggles; easily hitconfirmed into Super even if only the 2nd hit connects as a frame trap; higher KD advantage when juggled into

title.name: Tiger Hang
title.notes: Can use Drive Rush for a corner throw loop with 4f timing on standard characters; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

title.name: Tiger Carry
title.notes: Side switch; no true oki after throwing opponent into the corner, but can pressure with Tiger Shot, MK/HK Knee, or spaced Drive Rush normals; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

title.name: Tiger Punishment
title.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Impact|Drive Impact]]. Airborne connect gives spinning juggle state on Punish Counter or corner Wall Splat with variable height.

title.name: Tiger Twin Babel (Block)
title.notes: Performed by inputting 6HPHK during blockstun; 5f extra recovery on hit; freezes the screen for 4f during startup; see [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Reversal|Drive Reversal]].

title.name: Tiger Twin Babel (Recovery)
title.notes: Performed by holding 6HPHK on wakeup; 5f extra recovery on hit; does not have any screen freeze except for a 12f freeze when interacting with the opponent's throw hitbox (this also refunds 1 Drive bar after punishing the throw); see [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Reversal|Drive Reversal]].

title.name: Drive Parry
title.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Parry|Drive Parry]]. Perfect Parrying has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack. Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 11 frames of recovery. After a Perfect Parry, all Super Gauge gain is reduced by 20% and Drive Gauge gain/damage is reduced by 50% for the entire punish combo.

title.name: Parry Drive Rush
title.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Rush|Drive Rush]]; 1-bar version performed out of a Parry; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation (if performed immediately after a successful Parry, only 1+8 startup); the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties; Distance: 0.546 (min/throw), 1.869 (min/block), 3.156 (max/final DR frame)

title.name: Drive Rush Cancel
title.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Rush|Drive Rush]]; 3-bar version performed on hit/block from a cancelable normal; can also be performed on whiff from cancelable light normals; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation; the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties

title.name: Low Tiger Shot
title.notes: Fast 1-hit projectile (speed is not variable); can hit crouching opponents unlike MP/HP High Tiger Shot; has extremely long recovery compared to most projectiles (more vulnerable to jumps, Drive Impact, projectile invuln moves, and Perfect Parry + Drive Rush); the projectile travels low to the ground, preventing many low profile attacks from avoiding it (e.g. Kimberly's slide, A.K.I.'s Sinister Slide); Sagat is in a crouching state for the entire move duration with crouch-sized hurtboxes; puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state; extends a slight projectile invincible arm hurtbox on frames 16-43 and a leg hurtbox on frames 14-50; topmost hurtboxes are vulnerable only to air attacks on frames 1-50 (more vulnerable to jump-ins despite crouching) and are projectile invincible on frames 1-31 to avoid High Tiger Shot (frames 32-50 are still vulnerable)

title.name: Low Tiger Shot
title.notes: Fast 2-hit OD projectile; can hit crouching opponents; can be used while a meterless Tiger Shot is still on-screen; the projectile travels low to the ground, preventing many low profile attacks from avoiding it (e.g. Kimberly's slide, A.K.I.'s Sinister Slide); Sagat is in a crouching state for the entire move duration with crouch-sized hurtboxes; puts opponents into a limited juggle state; extends a slight projectile invincible arm hurtbox on frames 16-37 and a leg hurtbox on frames 12-44; topmost hurtboxes are vulnerable only to air attacks on frames 1-44 (more vulnerable to jump-ins despite crouching) and are projectile invincible on frames 1-29 to avoid High Tiger Shot (frames 30-44 are still vulnerable); starter scaling does not apply if only the 2nd hit connects (e.g. clashing with 1-hit OD fireball or breaking Drive Impact armor)

title.name: High Tiger Shot
title.notes: Medium speed 1-hit projectile (comparable to a shoto HP fireball, but much slower than Sagat's other projectiles); whiffs on crouching opponents unless they extend an upward hurtbox; puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state (useful juggle tool after 4HP launch); has much better recovery and frame advantage than the Low version; can be used meaty on the opponent's wakeup even if they crouch block, but the opponent can input a crouching button to avoid blocking the projectile; extends a slight projectile invincible arm hurtbox on frames 18-32 and a leg hurtbox on frames 18-42

title.name: High Tiger Shot
title.notes: Fast 1-hit projectile; whiffs on crouching opponents unless they extend an upward hurtbox; puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state; has much better recovery and frame advantage than the Low version; can be used meaty on the opponent's wakeup even if they crouch block, but the opponent can input a crouching button to avoid blocking the projectile; extends a slight projectile invincible arm hurtbox on frames 14-28 and a leg hurtbox on frames 14-42

title.name: High Tiger Shot
title.notes: Extremely fast 3-hit OD projectile; slow startup but very powerful in fireball wars once released; can hit crouching opponents unlike the MP/HP High Tiger Shots; can be used while a meterless Tiger Shot is still on-screen; excellent frame advantage on hit or block; puts opponents into a limited juggle state; can juggle into SA1 from anywhere on the screen, and into another OD High Tiger Shot from fullscreen (or at close range vs. cornered opponents); extends a slight projectile invincible arm hurtbox on frames 25-41 and a leg hurtbox on frames 25-48; starter scaling does not apply if only the 3rd hit connects (e.g. clashing with 2-hit OD fireball or breaking Drive Impact armor)

title.name: Tiger Uppercut
title.hitboxes: SF6_Sagat_623lp_hitbox_preview.png, SF6_Sagat_623lp_hitbox_1.png, SF6_Sagat_623lp_hitbox_2.png, SF6_Sagat_623lp_hitbox_3.png, SF6_Sagat_623p_hitbox_4.png, SF6_Sagat_623lp_hitbox_5.png
title.notes: Sagat's primary anti-air with fast startup but short range; deals 300 less damage on active frames 4-10; cannot hit cross-up; puts opponent into a limited juggle state (allows SA3 cancel on active frames 1-2 only)

title.name: Tiger Uppercut
title.hitboxes: SF6_Sagat_623mp_hitbox_preview.png, SF6_Sagat_623p_hitbox_1.png, SF6_Sagat_623p_hitbox_2.png, SF6_Sagat_623mp_hitbox_3.png, SF6_Sagat_623p_hitbox_4.png, SF6_Sagat_623p_hitbox_5.png
title.notes: Much more range than LP version for mid-range anti airs and more consistent cancels from light normals; much slower startup (limits usefulness in many juggles); forward movement makes Sagat more likely to whiff under an opponent's jump-in; deals 600 less damage on active frames 4-10; cannot hit cross-up; puts opponent into a limited juggle state (allows SA3 cancel on active frames 1-2 only)

title.name: Tiger Uppercut
title.hitboxes: SF6_Sagat_623hp_hitbox_preview.png, SF6_Sagat_623p_hitbox_1.png, SF6_Sagat_623p_hitbox_2.png, SF6_Sagat_623hp_hitbox_3.png, SF6_Sagat_623p_hitbox_4.png, SF6_Sagat_623p_hitbox_5.png
title.notes: Extremely long range but slow startup (cannot combo naturally from light normals, but works well as a combo ender from 5LK whiff punish); deals 600 less damage on active frames 3-10; cannot hit cross-up; can be charged into a much stronger version by holding the button 24 frames; releasing the button early results in uncharged 623HP with 8f startup after release; if partially charged, the Air Invuln is 1-9f then 1-11f after the button is released, and the airborne frames are 12-57f after the button is released; puts opponent into a limited juggle state (allows SA3 cancel on active frames 1-2 only)

title.name: Tiger Uppercut (hold)
title.hitboxes: SF6_Sagat_623hp_hold_hitbox_preview.png, SF6_Sagat_623hp_hold_hitbox_1.png, SF6_Sagat_623hp_hold_hitbox_2.png, SF6_Sagat_623hp_hitbox_3.png, SF6_Sagat_623p_hitbox_4.png, SF6_Sagat_623p_hitbox_5.png
title.notes: Button must be held 24f for fully charged version; causes a wall splat on hit that leaves Sagat at a fixed distance (no follow-up combo); deals 1100 less damage on active frames 4-10 with no wallsplat animation (KD +29 on active frame 4); cannot hit cross-up; 1st hit puts opponent into a limited juggle state (allows SA3 cancel on active frames 1-3 only)

title.name: Tiger Uppercut
title.hitboxes: SF6_Sagat_623pp_hitbox_preview.png, SF6_Sagat_623pp_hitbox_1.png, SF6_Sagat_623pp_hitbox_2.png, SF6_Sagat_623pp_hitbox_3.png, SF6_Sagat_623pp_hitbox_4.png, SF6_Sagat_623pp_hitbox_5.png, SF6_Sagat_623pp_hitbox_6.png, SF6_Sagat_623pp_hitbox_7.png
title.notes: Sagat's main reversal; slightly slower than most OD reversals (can be safejabbed by Dee Jay 5LP but is otherwise reliable); deals less damage if it connects on later active frames (1st hit deals significant portion of overall damage); cannot hit cross-up

title.name: Tiger Knee Crush
title.notes: Shortest horizontal distance; no front leg hurtbox on frames 10-12 (helps avoid low pokes); 3f extra landing recovery when absorbed by armor; much better block advantage (up to +2) when spaced at max range; active frames 2-10 whiff on crouching opponents; this means that if the Knee is spaced out so the first active frame whiffs, it always connects on the last active frame vs. crouching opponents (+2 on block, 623LP juggle on hit); puts opponent into a limited juggle state (in the corner, can juggle 623LP if the last active frame connects); blocked Knee causes a screen shake when Sagat has frame advantage; can be whiffed in some situations for a side switch vs. knocked down opponents

title.name: Tiger Knee Crush
title.notes: Medium horizontal distance; no front leg hurtbox on frames 14-15 (helps avoid low pokes); 3f extra landing recovery when absorbed by armor; much better block advantage (up to +3) when spaced at max range; active frames 2-9 whiff on crouching opponents; this means that if the Knee is spaced out so the first active frame whiffs, it connects on the 2nd to last active frame vs. crouching opponents (+2 on block, 623LP juggle on hit); puts opponent into a limited juggle state (in the corner, can juggle 623LP if the last 4 active frames connect); blocked Knee causes a screen shake when Sagat has frame advantage; can be whiffed in some situations for a side switch vs. knocked down opponents; sets up midscreen safe jump in certain juggle scenarios (e.g. after juggled 2MP~HP)

title.name: Tiger Knee Crush
title.hitboxes: SF6_Sagat_236hk_hitbox_preview.png, SF6_Sagat_236hk_hitbox_1.png, SF6_Sagat_236hk_hitbox_2.png, SF6_Sagat_236hk_hitbox_3.png, SF6_Sagat_236hk_hitbox_4.png, SF6_Sagat_236hk_hitbox_5.png
title.notes: Furthest horizontal distance; much better block advantage when spaced at max range; 2nd hit active frames 1-9 whiff on crouching opponents; this means that if the Knee is spaced out so the first hit whiffs, it connects on the 2nd to last active frame vs. crouching opponents (+4 on block); if the 1st hit is crouch blocked, opponent cannot switch to stand block the 2nd hit; puts opponent into a limited juggle state (in the corner, can juggle 623LP if the last 6 active frames connect, or 623MP / SA2 if the final active frame connects); blocked Knee causes a screen shake when Sagat has frame advantage; Sweet Spot occurs when juggling into the 3rd or 4th active frame, dealing 1300 damage (single hit) with a higher spinning juggle launch (ensures a follow-up juggle is possible even midscreen); the 2nd hit's extended Knee hitbox is too high to connect on standing characters on active frames 2-7 (tall characters: active frames 4-6); sets up corner safe jump in certain juggle scenarios (e.g. after juggled 2MP~HP)

title.name: Tiger Knee Crush
title.notes: 1st hit transitions to a different follow-up on hit, resulting in higher damage and a tumbling +66 knockdown (allows a 623LP juggle in the corner); if 1st hit whiffs, the next 2 hits deal 500 damage each and give a worse knockdown (only 1 hit at farthest ranges); safe on block but usually lands inside the opponent's throw range; 2nd hit active frames 2-10 whiff on crouching opponents; this means that if the Knee is spaced out, it connects on the last active frame vs. crouching opponents (+1 on block); unlike HK Knee, the opponent can switch to stand block after the first hit connects, leaving Sagat at -2; blocked Knee causes a screen shake when Sagat has frame advantage; later hits put opponent into a limited juggle state (in the corner, can juggle 623LP)

title.name: Tiger Nexus
title.notes: The only strength of 214K that can combo naturally from 5LP (3-light confirm can reach if point blank, but results in weaker mixup from ~6LK ender); does not frame trap when canceled on block (except from Light normals with Drive Rush or Burnout); combos into 6LK/6MK follow-ups; canceling into the 6HK follow-up leaves a 2f gap on hit / 9f gap on block (loses to 4f normals even in Burnout); Counter-hit/Punish Counter puts the opponent into a limited juggle state that allows the 6HK follow-up to combo; cannot perform follow-up attacks on whiff; opponents can beat all follow-ups with invincible reversals (but standard 6f reversals will not punish 214LK); if the opponent attempts to punish 214LK, the 6LK follow-up will frame trap on block (must delay 6LK vs. Burnout)

title.name: Tiger Nexus
title.notes: Combos into 6LK/6MK follow-ups; canceling into the 6HK follow-up leaves a 2f gap on hit / 9f gap on block (loses to 4f normals even in Burnout); Counter-hit/Punish Counter puts the opponent into a limited juggle state that allows the 6HK follow-up to combo; 214MK combos from canceled 2MP/2HP/5HK (these cancels also frame trap on block, with 2HP/5HK > 214MK being DI-safe); cannot perform follow-up attacks on whiff; lacks natural juggle potential but can juggle after DR~4HP (free juggle state) for a powerful 1-bar corner combo (DR~4HP > 214MK, 4HP > 236HK, 623MP); opponents can beat all follow-ups with invincible reversals (but standard 6f reversals will not punish 214MK); if the opponent attempts to punish 214MK, the 6LK follow-up will frame trap on block (must delay 6LK vs. Burnout)

title.name: Tiger Nexus
title.hitboxes: SF6_Sagat_214hk_hitbox_preview_1.png, SF6_Sagat_214hk_hitbox_preview_2.png, SF6_Sagat_214hk_hitbox_1.png, SF6_Sagat_214hk_hitbox_2.png, SF6_Sagat_214hk_hitbox_3.png, SF6_Sagat_214hk_hitbox_4.png
title.notes: Slow startup, only combos naturally from the first hit of 5HK (requires Drive Rush or Punish Counter to combo from 2HP, 4HP launch, or 5HK 2nd hit); 214HK cannot frame trap when canceled from a normal (except for Burnout + Drive Rush into 2MP/2HP/5HK); combos naturally into all follow-ups including 6HK; cancel into 6HK follow-up leaves a 7f gap on block (can frame trap 4f normals in Burnout); 2f extra recovery on Perfect Parry only; if the 1st hit is Perfect Parried, 2nd hit will interrupt any punish slower than 5f startup (trades with 6f attacks); cannot perform follow-up attacks if both hits whiff; carries Counter-hit/Punish Counter advantage through both hits; can juggle after DR~4HP (easier execution but less damaging than his MP Tiger Shot juggle sequence); opponents can beat all follow-ups with invincible reversals (but standard 6f reversals will not punish 214HK)

title.name: Tiger Nexus
title.hitboxes: SF6_Sagat_214hk_hitbox_preview_1.png, SF6_Sagat_214hk_hitbox_preview_2.png, SF6_Sagat_214hk_hitbox_1.png, SF6_Sagat_214hk_hitbox_2.png, SF6_Sagat_214hk_hitbox_3.png, SF6_Sagat_214hk_hitbox_4.png
title.notes: Similar to HK version but faster (can cancel from more starters like 2HP, 4HP launch, and 5HK 2nd hit); combos naturally into all follow-ups including 6HK; cancel into 6HK follow-up leaves a 7f gap on block (can frame trap 4f normals in Burnout); 2f extra recovery on Perfect Parry only; if the 1st hit is Perfect Parried, 2nd hit will interrupt any punish slower than 5f startup (trades with 6f attacks); cannot perform follow-up attacks if both hits whiff; carries Counter-hit/Punish Counter advantage through both hits; opponents can beat all follow-ups with invincible reversals (but standard 6f reversals will not punish 214KK)

title.name: Mighty Tiger
title.notes: Mixup tool that is unsafe on block; the threat of overhead makes ~6MK follow-up stronger while the threat of a frame trap makes ~6HK follow-up stronger; safe vs. Burnout but no longer frame traps (unless the 6LK input is delayed from 214LK/MK); puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state (e.g. after Counter-hit 214LK/MK); causes immediate knockdown on Counter-hit/Punish Counter; loses to reversal Drive Impact after blocked 214LK/MK (but safe after 214HK unless the 6LK input is delayed); if used from a 2-light chain, opponent is in range for Throw or 2LK~5LP chain (too far if starting with 3-light chain)

title.name: Mighty Tiger
title.notes: Mixup tool that is unsafe on block; the threat of overhead makes ~6MK follow-up stronger while the threat of a frame trap makes ~6HK follow-up stronger; safe vs. Burnout but no longer frame traps, making it a weak option; puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state (e.g. after DI wallsplat juggle); causes immediate knockdown on Counter-hit/Punish Counter; safe to reversal Drive Impact after blocked 214KK unless the 6LK input is delayed

title.name: Greedy Tiger
title.notes: Whiffs on crouching opponents; 2f extra recovery on whiff; puts opponent into a limited juggle state (only allows 623LP/PP juggle in the corner, or 623MP from CH/PC 214LK/MK launch); frame data vs. crouch from 214LK/214MK: -19 oH / -26 oB / KD +51 onPC launch; frame data vs. crouch from 214HK: -17 oH / -24 oB / -13 onPC (214HK keeps opponent crouching on Punish Counter); safe to reversal Drive Impact after blocked 214K; 5f blockstring gap after 214LK/MK (loses to reversal standing/crouching 4f normals); 3f blockstring gap after 214HK (frame trap vs. standing normals but still loses to reversal crouching normals); risky to attempt on block, but the threat of the overhead and frame trap from ~6LK follow-up can condition opponents to stand block or parry; opponent cannot walk back out of throw range on block

title.name: Greedy Tiger
title.notes: Whiffs on crouching opponents; 2f extra recovery on whiff; puts opponent into a limited juggle state (only allows 623LP/PP juggle in the corner); frame data vs. crouch from 214KK: -17 oH / -24 oB / -13 onPC; safe to reversal Drive Impact after blocked 214KK; 3f blockstring gap after 214KK (frame trap vs. standing normals but still loses to reversal crouching normals); risky to attempt on block, but the threat of the overhead and frame trap from ~6LK follow-up can condition opponents to stand block or parry; opponent cannot walk back out of throw range on block

title.name: Nova Tiger
title.notes: Connects on both standing/crouching opponents; launches opponent into a limited juggle state; only combos naturally from 214HK (requires juggle state for LK/MK versions, like a CH/PC Tiger Nexus); after blocked 214LK/MK, interruptible 9f blockstring gap (5f gap vs. Burnout, loses to 4f normals); after blocked 214HK, interruptible 7f blockstring gap (frame trap vs. Burnout); unsafe to reversal Drive Impact and susceptible to Perfect Parry on reaction (becomes DI-safe from 214HK if opponent delays it by 2f or longer); opponent is left inside throw range on block (but Sagat cannot shimmy an immediate throw tech)

title.name: Nova Tiger
title.notes: Connects on both standing/crouching opponents; launches opponent into a limited juggle state; combos naturally from 214KK; after blocked 214KK, interruptible 7f blockstring gap (frame trap vs. Burnout); unsafe to reversal Drive Impact and susceptible to Perfect Parry on reaction (becomes DI-safe from 214KK if opponent delays it by 2f or longer); opponent is left inside throw range on block (but Sagat cannot shimmy an immediate throw tech)

title.name: Tiger Cannon
title.notes: 8-hit Super projectile; can be used while OD Tiger Shot is on-screen (but not meterless Tiger Shot); fast travel speed allows it to be used in long range juggles; projectile speed is not affected by button strength

title.name: Savage Tiger
title.notes: Goes into one of 4 follow-ups after frame 76 on hit (depending on which direction is held while clutching the opponent); Sagat's best reversal Super (but is still slow enough to be baited by an opponent's safejab setup); can be used as a projectile counter from about half screen; Sagat has travel time throughout the active frames (takes longer than SA3 to reach full range)

title.name:
Savage Tiger Raid
(Damage)
title.notes: Highest damage variant of SA2; leaves the opponent far away with no oki; has significant corner carry (over 1/2 screen)

title.name:
Savage Tiger Zenith
(Launcher)
title.notes: Launches the opponent into a limited juggle state (juggle counter is always set to 2 regardless of previous juggle state); Hard Knockdown if the opponent hits the ground instead of being juggled; useful for corner carry, building back some Super gauge, and getting a good mix of damage + oki (but usually results in less damage than Tiger Raid)

title.name:
Savage Tiger Pendulum
(Side Switch)
title.notes: Switches sides with opponent; leaves the opponent far away with no oki

title.name:
Savage Tiger Stomp
(Oki)
title.notes: Leaves the opponent close to Sagat for stronger oki than other versions; can walk up for a strike/throw/shimmy mixup; gives enough advantage for an immediate 6MP overhead

title.name: Tiger Vanquisher
title.notes: Cinematic time regenerates ~1.9 Drive bars for Sagat; only 2f startup when canceled from Tiger Uppercut; can be used as a projectile counter from about half screen; slow startup makes it a risky wakeup reversal option; total damage scaling from anti-air Tiger Uppercut is 20-40% depending on which strength was used (weaker strength gives higher scaling)

title.name: Tiger Vanquisher (CA)
title.notes: Available at 25% HP or below; cinematic time regenerates ~1.9 Drive bars for Sagat; only 2f startup when canceled from Tiger Uppercut; can be used as a projectile counter from about half screen; slow startup makes it a risky wakeup reversal option; total damage scaling from anti-air Tiger Uppercut is 20-40% depending on which strength was used (weaker strength gives higher scaling)

title.name: Neutral Taunt
title.notes: "If you desire the throne, stand tall against the emperor."

title.name: Forward Taunt
title.notes: "The emperor's strength is peerless. However, it would be my pleasure to test your strength!"

title.name: Back Taunt
title.notes: "If you're on a path to attain strength, show me your worth!"
